title: genista tridentata subsp. rhiphea
intro: Genista tridentata subsp. rhiphea, also known as Spiny broom or Rhiphea broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This shrub-like plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Genista tridentata subsp. rhiphea are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe coughs, and support respiratory health, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the flowers and leaves, which can be used as a tea or infused oil for topical applications.
